content.text:
Please handle one small real repo docs/infra maintenance task.
Use the appropriate specialist to implement the change, then request a quick reviewer check before the final response.
Keep scope tight, ignore unrelated edits by others, and do not create proof markers or synthetic evidence files.
Required change: in docs/CURRENT_ARCHITECTURE.md add one brief sentence stating that invalid non-numeric CODEX_UI_PORT falls back to 57525 before UI_URL is built.
Do not modify unrelated files unless strictly necessary for this documentation update.

Please handle one small real repo docs/infra maintenance task.

Do not modify unrelated files unless strictly necessary for this documentation update.

item.text: 依頼内容を確認しました。`docs/CURRENT_ARCHITECTURE.md` の該当箇所を確認しつつ、実装は specialist に委譲し、その後 reviewer に短い確認を入れます。

item.aggregatedOutput: 14:- The UI is local-first and defaults to port `57525`.
fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git

item.text: 該当ドキュメントの該当行を特定しています。`CODEX_UI_PORT` と `UI_URL` の説明位置を見て、1文だけ差し込める場所を確定します。

item.aggregatedOutput:
1:# CURRENT_ARCHITECTURE
2:
3:Updated: 2026-03-07
4:
5:## 1) Purpose
6:
7:This document is the active architecture spec for the Codex App Server integration harness.
8:- Current-state architecture belongs here.
9:- Historical implementation logs belong in `docs/ARCHITECTURE_CHANGELOG.md`.
10:
11:## 2) Active Execution Path
12:
13:- Interactive execution stays on standard Codex through `POST /api/exec`.
14:- The UI is local-first and defaults to port `57525`.
15:- `default` is the default exec agent and the canonical collaboration-first parent entrypoint for end-to-end tasks.
16:- `start_codex_ui.bat` applies safer Windows launcher defaults only when env vars are unset:
17:  - `CODEX_EXECUTION_PROFILE=full-runtime`
18:  - `CODEX_REQUEST_USER_INPUT_POLICY=blocked`
19:  - `CODEX_PARENT_DISPATCH_GUARD_MODE=enforce`
20:  - `CODEX_PARENT_DISPATCH_GUARD_MAX_RETRIES=1`
21:- `server.js` now infers the non-interactive `request-user-input` fallback as `blocked` when `CODEX_REQUEST_USER_INPUT_POLICY` is unset.
22:- `server.js` owns HTTP APIs, app-server protocol handling, evidence capture, replay, eval, and SLO surfaces.
23:- `web/` owns the browser UI and uses the standard exec/runtime APIs.
24:
25:## 3) Collaboration-First Agent Topology
26:
27:- Parent roles:
28:  - `default`
29:    - only general-purpose parent role
30:    - config posture: `sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"`, `approval_policy = "never"`
31:    - owns end-to-end orchestration, specialist delegation, and final doc sync
32:  - `intake`
33:    - Step 1/2-only parent planner
34:    - config posture: `sandbox_mode = "read-only"`, `approval_policy = "never"`
35:    - locks baseline/over-delivery contract and dispatch criteria, then hands off
36:  - `release_manager`
37:    - Step 4/5-only parent gate
38:    - config posture: `sandbox_mode = "read-only"`, `approval_policy = "never"`
39:    - reviews child evidence, verifies doc sync, and decides PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED
40:- Specialist child roles:
41:  - `frontend_worker`
42:  - `backend_worker`
43:  - `infra_worker`
44:  - `tester`
45:  - `reviewer`
46:  - `explorer`
47:- Collaboration rule:
48:  - implementation work routes to child specialists instead of parent direct execution
49:  - `intake` and `release_manager` are focused parent overlays, not interchangeable default parents
50:  - unresolved user decisions must surface as `NEEDS_INPUT`/`BLOCKED`; parent roles must not auto-answer approval-boundary questions
51:- Retired runtime role:
52:  - `worker`
53:  - no longer configured in `.codex/config.toml` and must not appear in normal dispatch plans
54:  - `POST /api/exec` rejects `worker` and scoped aliases such as `worker@chat-legacy` as unconfigured runtime targets
55:  - retained only as a legacy governance contract artifact for bounded compatibility audits and explicit parent-override interpretation
56:
57:## 4) Machine-Readable Contracts
58:
59:- Agent governance:
60:  - `scripts/config/agent_governance_contracts.json`
61:  - defines parent agents, scope paths, read-only and verification-only roles, plus legacy `worker` audit gating
62:- Turn lifecycle:
63:  - `scripts/config/harness_contract_spec.json`
64:  - defines `in_progress -> completed|interrupted|failed`, terminal event `turn/completed`, and turn-to-task-outcome bridge rules
65:- Task outcome taxonomy:
66:  - `scripts/config/task_outcome_contract.json`
67:  - defines `COMPLETED`, `BLOCKED`, `NEEDS_INPUT`, `FAILED_VALIDATION`, `PARTIAL`
68:
69:## 5) Runtime Surfaces
70:
71:- `GET /api/runtime`
72:  - runtime snapshot, latest turn, governance policy, turn contract, task outcome contract, eval/replay/SLO capability summary
73:- `POST /api/exec`
74:  - authenticated JSON exec endpoint with NDJSON streaming turn events
75:  - defaults to `default` unless the request selects another configured role
76:  - rejects unconfigured agent targets such as retired `worker` and `worker@...` scoped aliases
77:  - idempotency key may be supplied by header or body, but header/body mismatch is rejected with `400` before a claim is created
78:  - duplicate idempotency reuse with the same key but a mismatched effective request hash returns `409` (`idempotency_request_hash_mismatch`)
79:  - duplicate in-flight idempotency claims return `409`; resolved duplicates return the stored outcome snapshot with `200`
80:  - carries request-scoped runtime posture such as sandbox, approval, and request-user-input settings
81:- `GET /api/exec/idempotency/:key`
82:  - returns the current idempotency lifecycle snapshot for a claimed exec request
83:  - exposes `running`, terminal states, and `released` when a claim was closed before a terminal outcome was recorded
84:- `GET /api/eval/suites`
85:- `GET /api/eval/history`
86:- `POST /api/eval/run`
87:  - accepts opt-in eval probe persistence via `persistProbeResultsToMemory` or legacy alias `persistProbeResults`
88:  - when enabled, synthetic probe outcomes are appended into harness execution memory through `persistProbeResultsToMemory` / `persistProbeResults`
89:- `GET /api/replay/turns`
90:- `GET /api/replay/turn/:turnId`
91:- `POST /api/replay/turn`
92:- `GET /api/slo/status`
93:- `GET /api/conversation/runtime`
94:- `POST /api/conversation/direct`
95:
96:## 6) Evidence and Persistence
97:
98:- Turn artifacts are written under `logs/turns/` with `manifest.json` plus events/items/diff/stdout/stderr artifacts.
99:- The turn artifact manifest now records:
100:  - terminal turn status
101:  - terminal task outcome status and reason
102:  - turn/task-outcome bridge validation surface through the runtime contracts
103:  - approval audit records
104:  - execution observed signals
105:- Harness memory is stored in `logs/harness_execution_memory.json` by default and can be redirected with `CODEX_HARNESS_MEMORY_PATH`.
106:- Eval run history is stored in `logs/eval_runs.jsonl` by default and can be redirected with `CODEX_EVAL_HISTORY_PATH`.
107:- Replay memory, idempotency snapshots, and latest turn snapshots carry `taskOutcomeStatus` and `taskOutcomeReason`.
108:- Idempotency snapshots also retain request metadata, lifecycle status, and response-close disposition for duplicate/replay inspection.
109:- `POST /api/eval/run` probe cases can now persist synthetic execution-memory records when `persistProbeResultsToMemory` or `persistProbeResults` is enabled.
110:- `scripts/generate_runtime_proof.js` is the isolated proof generator for this capability. Its verified proof bundle shape is:
111:  - `logs/proofs/runtime-proof-*/harness_execution_memory.json`
112:  - `logs/proofs/runtime-proof-*/eval_runs.jsonl`
113:  - `logs/proofs/runtime-proof-*/turns/` via `CODEX_TURN_ARTIFACTS_DIR`
114:  - `logs/proofs/runtime-proof-*/runtime_proof_summary.json`
115:  - live dispatch proof file: `logs/proofs/runtime-proof-*/live_dispatch_proof.md`
116:
117:## 7) Current Completion Gates
118:
119:- `server.js` or `scripts/` changes:
120:  - run `node scripts/app_server_smoke_test.js`
121:- Skill assignment or skill package changes:
122:  - run `node scripts/skill_portfolio_audit.js`
123:- Eval / workflow policy changes:
124:  - run `node scripts/eval_replay_api_smoke_test.js`
125:- Governance/runtime posture changes in `.codex/` or parent-role docs:
126:  - verify parent role config posture and role-boundary wording stay aligned across `.codex/config.toml`, `.codex/agents/*.toml`, and `docs/AGENT_OPERATING_RULES.md`
127:- Spec sync before `COMPLETED`:
128:  - update this file
129:  - append a matching entry to `docs/ARCHITECTURE_CHANGELOG.md`
130:
131:## 8) Current Residual Risks
132:
133:- Request-user-input posture is enforced primarily through runtime request metadata and governance docs; there is no separately documented per-agent `config.toml` key for that posture today.
134:- The server-level inferred non-interactive request-user-input default is now `blocked`, and the launcher matches that posture, so flows that intentionally rely on `auto-default` or `auto-empty` must opt in explicitly via env override.
135:- Context and evidence discipline are stronger, but context/memory promotion is still not fully machine-enforced.
136:- The legacy `worker` contract still exists for compatibility interpretation, even though runtime selection now rejects it.
137:- Default eval coverage is now workflow-aware, but broader scenario depth is still limited by the small baseline suite.
138:- Some lower-tier operator documents remain mixed-language.
